required_facts: The requested reusable skill package is `api_contract_drift_check`.
The request mode is creation of a new reusable Soulforge skill package; there is no existing skill package.
The workflow should decide that the request is reusable and should proceed to tracked package drafting.
The target behavior is public-safe API contract drift review from redacted packets.
The skill must compare claimed OpenAPI, generated TypeScript, route-handler, test-failure, and owner-note drift.
The skill must separate confirmed evidence, assumptions or likely impact, missing proof, blocked checks, likely owners, remediation order, and boundary warnings.
Runtime inputs may include redacted packet fields such as `openapi_changed_endpoints`, `generated_type_diff_summary`, `route_handler_touchpoints`, `test_failures`, and `owner_notes`.
Actual API specs, customer endpoint names, production logs, credentials, private incident details, `_workspaces`, `_workmeta`, and `private-state` material must stay out of tracked skill canon.
The proposed tracked package belongs under `.registry/skills/api_contract_drift_check/`.

Expected tracked package structure must include `skill.yaml` and `README.md`; optional `codex/SKILL.md`, public-safe templates, references, or scripts may be proposed when their owner boundary is clear.
Specific template, reference, script, or sync command names are examples unless the fixture explicitly requires them.
A helper script is optional and acceptable only if it works on synthetic or redacted packet files and does not encode project-specific endpoint names.
The resource bundle review must distinguish runtime inputs from tracked references, templates, and optional scripts.
The boundary review must confirm canon, optional bridge, runtime input, and local install ownership remain separate.
The install sync request must prepare a local mirror sync handoff but must not claim sync was performed unless direct execution evidence is present.
The smoke path must use or describe a tiny synthetic packet with two endpoints, one generated type mismatch, one route handler note, and one failing test name.
The smoke check must verify evidence, assumptions, owner handoff, remediation order, blockers or next checks, and boundary warnings.
The release review must state whether the package is ready, conditional, or blocked, and must identify the smallest safe next step.
Claims about created files, local sync, smoke execution, or release readiness must be classified as performed, proposed, conditional, blocked, or not-run based on explicit evidence.
critical_failures: Missing any of the seven required output groups.
Treating actual API specs, customer endpoint names, production logs, credentials, private incident details, `_workspaces`, `_workmeta`, or `private-state` as tracked package content.
Inventing real customer/project endpoint names or private source details instead of using synthetic or redacted placeholders.
Claiming that local installed mirror sync or a smoke run was performed when the candidate did not perform it.
Placing runtime packet archives or project-local run truth under `.workflow` or tracked skill canon.
Failing to separate evidence from assumptions or likely impact.
Failing to distinguish tracked package files from runtime inputs.
Requiring a helper script that embeds project-specific endpoint logic or non-public runtime data.
Rejecting an otherwise valid candidate only because it uses different public-safe template/reference names or does not cite the golden's exact sync command.
Making the workflow output a generic API review note instead of an author_skill_package lane output.
Contradicting the fixture by saying an existing package already exists.
boundary_rules: Candidate prompts must not receive the golden output or these criteria.
All candidate outputs must be public-safe and synthetic.
Tracked package recommendations may name paths under `.registry/skills/api_contract_drift_check/` but must not create real runtime packet content.
Boundary-sensitive paths must be treated as prohibited content sources, not examples to read.
Install handoff may cite the sync procedure as a handoff, but not as an action already completed.
Smoke status may be proposed or not-run unless the candidate actually performs a safe smoke.
usability_checks: The output is concise enough for a release reviewer but complete enough to guide the package author.
Each output group has a clear decision, boundary note, or next action appropriate to its workflow step.
The package draft names canonical files and separates `skill.yaml`, README, optional `codex/` bridge, templates/references/scripts if any, and runtime inputs.
The resource bundle review makes clear why real packets and project data stay local.
The boundary review reports pass/fix status and smallest safe correction if needed.
The install sync request is procedural and avoids claiming completed sync.
The smoke check defines an evaluable synthetic fixture and pass condition.
The release review preserves conditionality if package files or smoke evidence are not actually present.
Alternate wording and section structure are acceptable when all seven output groups and behavior requirements are satisfied.

measurement_eval_policy.candidate_output_quality: Evaluate from candidate output text and hard gates only.
measurement_eval_policy.token_cost_efficiency: Use CLI telemetry probe values only after quality pass; do not penalize subagent candidates for missing token metadata.
measurement_eval_policy.wall_clock_time: Use CLI telemetry probe values only after quality pass; do not penalize subagent candidates for missing wall-clock metadata.
measurement_eval_policy.stability_across_reruns: Score as unknown until finalists are rerun; do not fail first-pass candidates solely for missing stability data.
measurement_eval_policy.false_execution_claims: Penalize or fail claims that files were created, sync was run, smoke was executed, or release was verified unless direct evidence is supplied.
